# Lynx PDF Studio

> Free, local, deterministic PDF automation. You describe a document job once in an
> OpenPDF Workflow file (`*.opw.yaml` — plain YAML, one line per step); a fixed engine
> renders the PDF on the user's machine. Ships as a VS Code extension with a bundled
> MCP server, so any coding agent can author and validate workflows without the
> document ever entering the model's context: the agent writes the workflow file, the
> engine processes the PDF locally.

Key facts: 80 operations across 13 categories (pages, stamps, metadata, extraction,
redaction, forms, attachments, encryption, optimization, convert to/from PDF, AI,
signatures). Fills 12 pre-mapped US government forms. Creates fillable AcroForms from
Word/Markdown templates. Batch = a glob in `inputs`. Pages are 1-based. The workflow
file is the source of truth; the PDF is a build artifact.

## Reference

- [Operations reference (Markdown)](https://pdf.lynxdi.com/operations.md): every
  operation with usage notes and a runnable example.
- [Security & trust model](https://pdf.lynxdi.com/trust/): what is protected, and
  exactly when data can leave the machine.

## Install

- [VS Code Marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=LynxDI.lynxdi-pdf-studio):
  install "Lynx PDF Studio", then run "PDF Studio: Initialize Project". The extension
  can generate CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / GEMINI.md so your agent learns the workflow
  format, and "Set Up MCP for This Workspace" exposes deterministic OPW helpers
  (validate / compile / diff / operations / scaffold).
- [Public repo](https://github.com/LynxDI/pdf-studio): README, changelog, license.
